home.title: Smart Agriculture
home.subtitle: Starting Simply
home.description1: PlantGuard protects your crops with advanced AI.
home.description2: Early detection of pathologies and real-time monitoring of field health.
home.monitor_desc: Analyze data from Drones/Cameras to early detect stressed areas on a large scale.
home.doctor_desc: Accurately diagnose leaf diseases and receive immediate treatment protocols.
home.footer: © 2026 PlantGuard AI • Smart crop protection solutions

monitor.title: Garden Monitoring
monitor.subtitle: Analyze Live Cam & Drone data in real-time.
monitor.processing_desc: The system is scanning data. You can visit other pages while waiting.
monitor.disclaimer: Note: Analysis results are based on remote imagery. In low light or shaky camera conditions, accuracy may decrease. Please cross-reference with Plant Doctor data for the most accurate results.
monitor.camera_desc: Enter URL and press Start to scan the field
monitor.error_upload: Error analyzing video. Please try again.
monitor.error_reconnecting: Connection interrupted. Attempting to reconnect automatically...

doctor.title: Plant Doctor
doctor.subtitle: Diagnose pathogens through artificial intelligence.
doctor.recommendation_desc: This technique is based on deep learning models. Results may vary depending on image quality. Please consult an expert before applying large-scale plant protection chemicals.
doctor.server_error: Server encountered an issue processing the image. Please try again later.

history.title: Diagnosis History
history.subtitle: Review all your crop diagnosis records
history.start_first: Start by diagnosing your first leaf
history.delete_confirm_msg: Are you sure you want to delete this diagnosis record? This action cannot be undone.

detail.title: Diagnosis Details
detail.subtitle: Detailed information about the crop diagnosis
detail.expert_note_desc: Results are stored from a previous session. Disease status may have changed over time.

auth.login_desc: Log in to continue protecting your garden
auth.login_required_desc: Please log in to save diagnosis results to your personal history.
auth.register_desc: Start your professional crop care journey
auth.error_email_invalid: Invalid email. Please enter a correctly formatted email.
auth.error_password_invalid: Password must be at least 6 characters, including letters and numbers.
auth.error_name_short: Full name must be at least 2 characters
auth.error_email_exists: This email is already in use. Please choose another email.
auth.error_login_failed: Incorrect email or password. Please try again.

Apple Healthy.name: Healthy Apple Leaf
Apple Healthy.desc: Apple leaf in its best growth state.

treatments.identification_guide: Leaf maintains smooth green color, no abnormal signs found.
treatments.action: Continue regular care, water steadily

treatments.identification_guide: Entire canopy looks uniform, no abnormal yellow leaf drop.
treatments.action: Regularly check for pests on the underside of leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Growth points develop well, healthy young shoots.
treatments.action: Maintain macro, medium, and micro-nutrient fertilization regime

Apple Scab.name: Apple Scab
Apple Scab.desc: Disease caused by the fungus Venturia inaequalis, severely affecting apple yield.

treatments.identification_guide: A few small olive-colored spots appear on the underside of leaves.
treatments.action: Clean up fallen leaves, water at the base

treatments.identification_guide: Many brown-black spots enlarge on both sides of the leaf, leaf begins to curl slightly.
treatments.action: Spray antifungal when buds open

treatments.identification_guide: Dense ulcers make leaves wrinkled, dry, and fall early in mass.
treatments.action: Apply a strict spraying schedule

Apple Rust.name: Cedar Apple Rust
Apple Rust.desc: Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae fungus causes prominent orange spots.
symptoms: Bright orange spots on the upper leaf surface
Small fungal spikes under the leaf

treatments.identification_guide: A few small lemon-yellow spots appear individually on the leaf surface.
treatments.action: Remove nearby alternate hosts (juniper trees)

treatments.identification_guide: Spots turn dark orange, small black dots begin to appear in the center of the lesion.
treatments.action: Spray when young leaves first emerge

treatments.identification_guide: Spore-bearing spike-like tubes grow under the leaf surface, leaves turn yellow and fall.
treatments.action: Use strong antifungal drugs

Bell Pepper Healthy.name: Healthy Bell Pepper Leaf
symptoms: No symptoms of wilting or spots

treatments.identification_guide: Uniform green leaves, not curled or with water-soaked spots.
treatments.action: Maintain stable soil moisture

treatments.identification_guide: Underside of leaves is clean, no insects or fungal spores.
treatments.action: Supplement nutrition to increase resistance

treatments.identification_guide: Plant grows straight, balanced foliage.
treatments.action: Ensure plant receives enough sunlight

Bell Pepper Leaf Spot.name: Bell Pepper Leaf Spot
Bell Pepper Leaf Spot.desc: Usually caused by Xanthomonas bacteria in hot and humid environments.
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots, later drying to brown

treatments.identification_guide: Small water-soaked spots appear at the leaf edge or underside.
treatments.action: Avoid watering directly onto leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread, with a light brown center and dark border, leaves begin to yellow.
treatments.action: Spray copper bactericidal preparations

treatments.identification_guide: Many leaves are scorched and dry, falling in mass, affecting both flowers and young fruit.
treatments.action: Uproot diseased plants, stop excessive nitrogen fertilization

Blueberry Healthy.name: Healthy Blueberry Leaf
Blueberry Healthy.desc: Analysis of healthy blueberry leaf state.
symptoms: Generally green and healthy, may have small natural scars

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves have characteristic variety color, shiny surface.
treatments.action: Regularly monitor soil pH (4.5-5.5)

treatments.identification_guide: Leaf margins are straight, not brown-scorched or curled.
treatments.action: Check for stem borers in main branches

treatments.identification_guide: Leaf veins are not abnormally yellow compared to the leaf blade.
treatments.action: Periodically supplement iron to keep leaves dark green

Cherry Healthy.name: Healthy Cherry Leaf
symptoms: Dark green leaves, even serrated margins

treatments.identification_guide: Dark green leaves, balanced, no tiny holes.
treatments.action: Water regularly in the morning to avoid standing water

treatments.identification_guide: Smooth stems and branches, no white or red mold.
treatments.action: Periodically apply organic fertilizer around the base

treatments.identification_guide: Shady canopy, no early leaf drop.
treatments.action: Lightly prune branches hidden in the canopy

Corn Healthy.name: Healthy Corn Leaf
Corn Healthy.desc: Corn leaf in its best growth state.

treatments.identification_guide: Uniform green leaves, no abnormal spots or stripes.
treatments.action: Maintain periodic fertilization and steady watering

treatments.identification_guide: Sturdy stem, no signs of stem borers.
treatments.action: Regularly check for early disease prevention

treatments.identification_guide: Corn whorl develops well, not twisted or yellowed.
treatments.action: Ensure reasonable planting density for ventilation

Corn Common Rust.name: Corn Common Rust
Corn Common Rust.desc: Disease caused by Puccinia sorghi fungus, usually appearing in wet conditions.
symptoms: Red-brown fungal pustules appear on both sides of the leaf

treatments.identification_guide: Only a few small spots scattered on some lower leaves.
treatments.action: Prune infected leaves to create ventilation

treatments.identification_guide: Fungal spots appear more, spreading to middle leaves, dense spot density.
treatments.action: Periodically spray antifungal drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Entire leaf covered by fungal pustules, leaves scorched and fall early.
treatments.action: Destroy severely infected plants, crop rotation

Corn Gray Leaf Spot.name: Corn Gray Leaf Spot
Corn Gray Leaf Spot.desc: Disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis fungus, very common in corn growing areas.
symptoms: Rectangular lesions elongated between two veins

treatments.identification_guide: New lesions are small elongated water-soaked streaks on low old leaves.
treatments.action: Crop rotation (with beans)

treatments.identification_guide: Distinct rectangular lesions appear on 50% of leaves.
treatments.action: Spray when the first lesions are seen

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions completely cover the leaf, scorching from bottom to top.
treatments.action: Use high concentration drugs or resistant varieties

Corn Northern Leaf Blight.name: Corn Northern Leaf Blight
Corn Northern Leaf Blight.desc: Disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum fungus in cool and humid weather.
symptoms: Large ulcers running along the leaf veins

treatments.identification_guide: A few small spindle-shaped lesions appear individually on old leaves.
treatments.action: Remove diseased basal leaves, clean weeds

treatments.identification_guide: Many long lesions spread to surrounding leaves, occupying about 25% of the leaf area.
treatments.action: Spray for fungal prevention

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions merge together, scorching the entire leaf, plant stunted.
treatments.action: Use disease-resistant varieties for the next season
treatments.product: Mancozeb combined with Trichoderma antagonist fungus

Corn Fall Army Worm.name: Corn Fall Armyworm
Corn Fall Army Worm.desc: Dangerous pest species capable of wide-scale destruction of food crops.

treatments.identification_guide: Small scratches on leaves, little frass in the whorl, low worm density.
treatments.action: Hand-pick worms, balanced fertilization

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves begin to have large holes, young worms appear, many whorls have frass.
treatments.action: Use bait traps, spray herbal extracts

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves eaten away, worms eat into the growth point killing the whorl, high worm density.
treatments.action: Spray specific chemical drugs for armyworms

Corn Lethal Necrotic.name: Maize Lethal Necrosis
Corn Lethal Necrotic.desc: Extremely dangerous disease caused by the combination of two viruses (MCMV and SCMV).

treatments.identification_guide: Plant shows signs of slight leaf yellowing at the tip or young leaf margins.
treatments.action: Immediately uproot suspected infected plants

treatments.identification_guide: Necrotic lesions begin to appear clearly along the veins, plant shows signs of dwarfing.
treatments.action: Control insect vectors (thrips, aphids)

treatments.identification_guide: Entire plant scorched dry from the top down, ears do not develop or rot.
treatments.action: Destroy the entire field if over 50% infected

Corn Magnesium Deficiency.name: Corn Magnesium Deficiency
Corn Magnesium Deficiency.desc: Nutritional disorder reducing the plant's photosynthetic capacity.
symptoms: Leaf margins may turn red or purple

treatments.identification_guide: Only the bottom old leaves have slight yellow stripes between green veins.
treatments.action: Supplement with foliar fertilizer containing Magnesium

treatments.identification_guide: Many leaves on the lower stem are deeply yellowed, margins start turning red/purple.
treatments.action: Apply basal fertilizer with high Magnesium content

treatments.identification_guide: Yellowing spreads near the top, old leaves completely die off.
treatments.action: Adjust soil pH, apply Dolomite lime

Corn Aphids.name: Maize Aphids
Corn Aphids.desc: Insects that suck plant sap and transmit viral diseases.
symptoms: Aphids densely packed in corn whorls and ears
Leaves curled and sticky with honeydew

treatments.identification_guide: A few small aphid clusters in leaf axils or whorls.
treatments.action: Use high-pressure water to wash away aphids

treatments.identification_guide: Aphids in large patches, leaves begin to deform and have sticky fluid.
treatments.action: Use natural enemies or herbal oils

treatments.identification_guide: Aphids cover the whorl and silk, sooty mold covers the leaves.
treatments.action: Use systemic insecticides

Corn Phosphorus Deficiency.name: Corn Phosphorus Deficiency
Corn Phosphorus Deficiency.desc: Lack of phosphorus causes poor growth and weak roots.
symptoms: Old leaves turn purple or reddish-purple

treatments.identification_guide: Only the margins of the lowest old leaves are slightly purple.
treatments.action: Spray phosphorus-rich foliar fertilizer

treatments.identification_guide: Purple color spreads across the entire old leaf, plant seems slightly shorter than normal.
treatments.action: Side-dress with phosphorus fertilizer

treatments.identification_guide: Many leaf layers are purple, stem is slender, roots poorly developed.
treatments.action: Periodically improve soil with organic fertilizer

Corn Physoderma Brown Spot.name: Physoderma Brown Spot
Corn Physoderma Brown Spot.desc: Disease often seen on corn stems and leaf veins under high humidity.
symptoms: Small yellow or purple-brown spots on leaf sheaths
Main veins have small raised nodules
Stems become weak and easily broken

treatments.identification_guide: A few small colorless spots appear on leaf sheaths and main veins.
treatments.action: Clean up crop residues after harvest

treatments.identification_guide: Spots turn distinct purple-brown, densely packed at stem nodes.
treatments.action: Reduce planting density, water reasonably

treatments.identification_guide: Disease spreads across stem and leaves, stem becomes brittle and easily breaks at nodes.
treatments.action: Use chemical drugs when the disease spreads widely

Corn Streak Virus.name: Maize Streak Virus
Corn Streak Virus.desc: Disease caused by virus transmitted by leafhoppers.
symptoms: Small light yellow streaks running along the leaf

treatments.identification_guide: Tiny yellow dots appear at the base of young leaves.
treatments.action: Destroy leafhopper vectors

treatments.identification_guide: Yellow streaks form clearly along veins, plant begins to grow slowly.
treatments.action: Use biological leafhopper control preparations

treatments.identification_guide: Top leaves completely yellow-streaked, plant much shorter than the rest of the field, no ears produced.
treatments.action: Uproot diseased plants, switch to MSV-resistant varieties

Grape Healthy.name: Healthy Grape Leaf
Grape Healthy.desc: Grape leaf in good development state.
symptoms: Dark green, succulent leaves, no rust

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves expand large, smooth green, leaf stalks strong without oil spots.
treatments.action: Balanced macro and micro-nutrient fertilization

treatments.identification_guide: No downy mildew or white powder sticking to hands when stroking leaves.
treatments.action: Water directly to the base, limit wetting leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Always observe branch crevices to prevent borer damage.
treatments.action: Periodically check for stem borer signs

Grape Black Rot.name: Grape Black Rot
Grape Black Rot.desc: Guignardia bidwellii fungus harms both grape leaves and fruit.
symptoms: Circular brown spots with dark black borders
Tiny black dots in the center of the lesion

treatments.identification_guide: A few single circular brown spots appear on young leaves.
treatments.action: Clean up fallen leaves and dried fruit

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions large and wide, clear black-brown border, appearing on young fruit clusters.
treatments.action: Spray antifungal immediately after flowering

treatments.identification_guide: Grapes completely rot black, leaves completely dropped affecting the next season.
treatments.action: Repeat spray periodically every 10-14 days

Grape Esca.name: Grape Esca
Grape Esca.desc: Disease caused by a fungal complex, damaging vascular tissues in the trunk and branches.
symptoms: Leaves have dry scorched patches between veins
Trunk has dark streaks along the stem

treatments.identification_guide: A few leaves have light yellow patches between veins, margins slightly dry.
treatments.action: Prune and destroy diseased branches

treatments.identification_guide: Many leaves scorched in a 'tiger-stripe' pattern, plant begins to weaken.
treatments.action: Periodically spray antifungal drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Main branches die suddenly (Apoplexy), leaves wither within days.
treatments.action: Uproot diseased plants, disinfect soil before replanting

Grape Leaf Blight.name: Grape Leaf Blight
Grape Leaf Blight.desc: Disease caused by Pseudocercospora vitis fungus, common in tropical regions.
symptoms: Red-brown lesions on the upper leaf surface
Gray fungal layer on the leaf underside

treatments.identification_guide: A few small red-brown spots appear on the upper surface of old leaves.
treatments.action: Clean up fallen leaves, create canopy ventilation

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread, clear gray spore layer on leaf underside.
treatments.action: Spray antifungal when the first lesions are seen

treatments.identification_guide: Entire leaf scorched brown and falls, affecting fruit quality.
treatments.action: Repeat spray every 10-14 days

Peach Healthy.name: Healthy Peach Leaf
Peach Healthy.desc: Normal growth state of peach leaves.

treatments.identification_guide: Elongated leaves, margins not punctured by insects.
treatments.action: Prevent leaf-eating insects with biological preparations

treatments.identification_guide: Even light green color, no white film on the leaf surface.
treatments.action: Hoe around the base to increase aeration

treatments.identification_guide: Branches and leaves reach out naturally, not twisted.
treatments.action: Prevent leaf curl disease when weather changes seasons
treatments.product: Spray Copper Sulfate at the end of winter

Peach Bacterial Spot.name: Peach Bacterial Spot
Peach Bacterial Spot.desc: Disease caused by Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni bacteria on peach leaves and fruit.
symptoms: Small angular brown-black spots on leaves

treatments.identification_guide: A few small brown spots appear on the underside of leaves.
treatments.action: Prune diseased leaves and branches

treatments.identification_guide: Many spots cause leaves to have holes, fruit has dark lesions.
treatments.action: Periodically spray copper-based antibacterial drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Mass leaf drop, severe fruit ulcers, plant weakens.
treatments.action: Use disease-resistant varieties for the next season

Potato Healthy.name: Healthy Potato Leaf
symptoms: Green, shiny leaves, no dark bruises

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves fully expanded, no circular brown-black spots.
treatments.action: Monitor pest insect density

treatments.identification_guide: Veins prominent, smooth leaf surface, margins not withered.
treatments.action: Hill up to increase tuber formation area

treatments.identification_guide: Plant looks sturdy, does not droop in the sun.
treatments.action: Ensure good drainage to avoid root rot

Potato Early Blight.name: Potato Early Blight
Potato Early Blight.desc: Caused by Alternaria solani fungus, attacking older leaves first.
symptoms: Black spots with concentric rings like a bullseye
Leaves turn yellow and fall early

treatments.identification_guide: Small circular lesions appear on the bottom-most leaves.
treatments.action: Maintain adequate fertilization, avoid nitrogen deficiency

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions enlarge, surrounded by a yellow halo, spreading to the middle leaf layer.
treatments.action: Drip irrigation, avoid wetting leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Entire foliage yellowed and scorched, stem wilted.
treatments.action: Apply broad-spectrum fungicides

Potato Late Blight.name: Potato Late Blight
Potato Late Blight.desc: Most dangerous disease on potatoes caused by Phytophthora infestans.
symptoms: Fine white mold layer on leaf underside when humid

treatments.identification_guide: Dark green, slightly water-soaked spots at the leaf edge.
treatments.action: Remove pathogens, destroy quickly

treatments.identification_guide: Wide rotten ulcers, white mold covering the leaf underside.
treatments.action: Spray preventive medicine immediately during rainy/humid weather

treatments.identification_guide: Stem rots black, entire leaf area decayed.
treatments.action: Use specific drugs of Cyazofamid line

treatments.identification_guide: Dark green trifoliate or pentafoliate leaves, no vein silvering.
treatments.action: Prune old branches that have fruited to concentrate nutrients

treatments.identification_guide: Young branches grow straight, no purple spots or white fungus.
treatments.action: Manage base moisture with mulch material

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves grow thick but with good air circulation.
treatments.action: Prevent leaf rust during rainy/humid periods

Soybean Healthy.name: Healthy Soybean Leaf
symptoms: Uniform green leaves, no holes from pests

treatments.identification_guide: Three leaflets uniform green, no tiny yellow dots from red spider mites.
treatments.action: Periodic phosphorus fertilization to enhance root development

treatments.identification_guide: Underside of leaves clean, leaf sheaths not covered by pink fungus.
treatments.action: Check for mealybugs in leaf axils

treatments.identification_guide: Soybean stem slender but tough, can withstand wind.
treatments.action: Ensure drainage ditches are always clear

Squash Powdery Mildew.name: Squash Powdery Mildew
Squash Powdery Mildew.desc: White powder-like layer covering squash leaves, reducing yield.
symptoms: White powdery layer covering the leaf surface
Leaves can be dry, yellow, and brittle

treatments.identification_guide: Only a few tiny white dots like powder on the leaf surface.
treatments.action: Spray a mixture of fresh milk and water (ratio 1:9)

treatments.identification_guide: About 1/2 of the leaf area covered by a thick white powdery layer.
treatments.action: Use biological preparations

treatments.identification_guide: White powder covers the entire leaf and stalk, leaf becomes dry, brittle, and dies early.
treatments.action: Use sulfur or triazole-based fungicides

Strawberry Healthy.name: Healthy Strawberry Leaf
Strawberry Healthy.desc: Strawberry leaf in a healthy green state.
symptoms: Shiny green leaves, margins not scorched black

treatments.identification_guide: Clustered green leaves, sharp margins, no dry brown spots.
treatments.action: Regularly clean weeds and old leaves around the base

treatments.identification_guide: No leaf rollers or diamondback moths found on the foliage.
treatments.action: Supplement Potassium fertilizer when the plant starts flowering

treatments.identification_guide: Strawberry runners (if any) grow strong, not black-tipped.
treatments.action: Line straw around the base so fruit doesn't touch the ground

Strawberry Leaf Scorch.name: Strawberry Leaf Scorch
Strawberry Leaf Scorch.desc: Disease caused by Diplocarpon earlianum fungus, causing strawberry leaves to scorch dry.
symptoms: Lesions enlarge causing leaves to scorch dry

treatments.identification_guide: A few small purple spots appear scattered on old leaves.
treatments.action: Clean up diseased leaves, keep garden ventilated

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread, leaf margins begin to dry and curl.
treatments.action: Periodically spray antifungal drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Most leaves are scorched and fall, plant remains weak.
treatments.action: Cut off all diseased leaves, spray specific drugs

Tomato Healthy.name: Healthy Tomato Leaf
symptoms: Green branches and leaves, good flowering/fruiting

treatments.identification_guide: Uniform green leaves, flat leaf blade, no mosaic or water-soaked oil spots.
treatments.action: Maintain periodic bio-organic fertilizer supplementation

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves grow symmetrically, no sucking insects on the underside.
treatments.action: Clean weeds competing for nutrients

treatments.identification_guide: Airy canopy, no unpleasant musty smell.
treatments.action: Prune branches to create ventilation and prevent mold

Tomato Early Blight.name: Tomato Early Blight
Tomato Early Blight.desc: Caused by Alternaria solani, creating concentric black spots.
symptoms: Black or brown spots with concentric rings
Usually starts from lower old leaves

treatments.identification_guide: Few brown spots with small rings appear on leaves near the ground.
treatments.action: Periodically spray biological preparations

treatments.identification_guide: Lesions spread up to middle leaf layers, yellow halo around lesions widens.
treatments.action: Prune leaves near the ground

treatments.identification_guide: Plant withers, leaves completely scorched black, thick mold.
treatments.action: Spray combination of systemic antifungal drugs

Tomato Late Blight.name: Tomato Late Blight
Tomato Late Blight.desc: Very dangerous disease, can kill an entire tomato field in days.
symptoms: White fungal mycelium under leaves when humid
Flowers and fruit rot soft black

treatments.identification_guide: Small water-soaked oil spots at the tip or near the stalk of leaves.
treatments.action: Carefully check initial seedlings

treatments.identification_guide: Black ulcers spread along stems and leaf stalks, white mycelium clearly visible.
treatments.action: Repeat spray every 7 days

treatments.identification_guide: Entire canopy soft, foul-smelling, fruit rots soft blue-black.
treatments.action: Use extremely strong systemic drugs

Tomato Bacterial Spot.name: Tomato Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small, hard, scabby black spots on leaves and fruit
Lesions spread quickly during heavy rain

treatments.identification_guide: Small black spots slightly scratched on the leaf back, not yet spread to leaf sheaths.
treatments.action: Use disease-free seeds, soak in warm water

treatments.identification_guide: Dense black spots, leaf margin scorching, plant slightly wilted.
treatments.action: Spray biological antibacterial drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Disease spreads to branches and young fruit, plant stem rots black, severe necrosis.
treatments.action: Spray mixture of Copper and Mancozeb

Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.name: Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.desc: Fungus causes many small spots, gray center with brown border.
symptoms: Many small circular gray-white spots with black borders
Leaves drop from base to top

treatments.identification_guide: Only a few faint gray spots scattered on a few single leaves.
treatments.action: Avoid watering leaves at night

treatments.identification_guide: Entire leaf peppered with dozens of small spots, leaf margins start curling dry.
treatments.action: Clean up mulch straw thoroughly

treatments.identification_guide: Basal leaves all fallen, disease spread near the top, plant thin and weak.
treatments.action: Use specific mold drugs

Tomato Mosaic Virus.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus (ToMV)
Tomato Mosaic Virus.desc: Virus spreads through seeds, tools, and insects.

treatments.identification_guide: Small part of young leaves has light green - dark green mottling.
treatments.action: Wash hands with soap after touching diseased plants

treatments.identification_guide: Lá mọc ra bị vặn xoắn, có đốm vàng loang lổ khắp tán.
treatments.action: Tiêu diệt côn trùng truyền bệnh rầy, rệp

treatments.identification_guide: Plant completely deformed, thread-like leaves, cannot flower or set fruit.
treatments.action: Uproot all diseased plants, disinfect tools
treatments.product: Stop cultivating Solanaceous crops for one season

Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV)
Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.desc: Virus transmitted by silverleaf whiteflies, causes leaf curling and yellowing.
symptoms: Distinct yellowing at the top, young leaves become small

treatments.identification_guide: Plant tip starts turning lemon yellow, leaves slightly thickened.
treatments.action: Use whitefly netting

treatments.identification_guide: Young leaves twisted tight, margins curled up like a cup, plant stops growing tall.
treatments.action: Spray mineral oil so whiteflies don't land

treatments.identification_guide: Squash tip matted and not developing, entire foliage yellowed.
treatments.action: Absolutely control whiteflies with strong drugs

Tomato Leaf Mold.name: Tomato Leaf Mold
Tomato Leaf Mold.desc: Disease caused by Fulvia fulva fungus in extremely high humidity.
symptoms: Light yellow spots on upper leaf surface, green/gray mold layer on underside
Leaves dry and drop but don't wilt

treatments.identification_guide: A few faint light yellow streaks on the upper leaf surface, flip to the underside to see fine gray fuzz.
treatments.action: Increase greenhouse ventilation

treatments.identification_guide: Mold sticks thick like gray velvet on the underside of most old leaves.
treatments.action: Use broad-spectrum antifungal drugs

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves dry black and fall in mass, disease spreads up to young leaves at the tip.
treatments.action: Need chemical drug intervention

Tomato Two Spotted Spider Mites.name: Tomato Two-Spotted Spider Mite
Tomato Two Spotted Spider Mites.desc: Mites suck sap causing leaves to discolor and dry.
symptoms: Pale leaves, thin spider webs on underside
Leaves scorched yellow across the field

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves have a few faint tiny white dots, look closely to see small mites moving on the back.
treatments.action: Spray water strongly on the leaf underside

treatments.identification_guide: Leaves clearly silver-white, thin web on the back, leaves slightly curled.
treatments.action: Use herbal oils or soap

treatments.identification_guide: Entire field scorched yellow, leaves dry and dead, mites cover the plant tips.
treatments.action: Use specific miticides
